title: The socialmixr package
text: Contact matrices computed small pipeline composable functions: survey[expr] – filter participants contacts. assign_age_groups() – impute missing ages assign age groups. weigh() – optionally apply participant weights. compute_matrix() – compute mean contact matrix. symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita() – optional post-processing. step separate function. POLYMOD data included package can loaded using example use generates contact matrix UK part POLYMOD study, age groups 0-1, 1-5, 5-15 15+ years. contains mean number contacts member age group (row) reported members another age group (column). assign_age_groups() prepares survey matrix computation. imputes participant contact ages available ranges, drops keeps rows missing ages (configurable via missing_participant_age missing_contact_age), adds age.group contact.age.group columns using age_limits supply: resulting survey object can inspected, subset, passed number weigh() calls compute_matrix().
code: data(polymod) polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 1, 5, 15)) |>   compute_matrix() #>  #> ── Contact matrix (4 age groups) ── #>  #> Ages: "[0,1)", "[1,5)", "[5,15)", and "[15,Inf)" #> Participants: 1011 #>  #>           contact.age.group #> age.group       [0,1)     [1,5)   [5,15) [15,Inf) #>   [0,1)    0.40000000 0.8000000 1.266667 5.933333 #>   [1,5)    0.11250000 1.9375000 1.462500 5.450000 #>   [5,15)   0.02450980 0.5049020 7.946078 6.215686 #>   [15,Inf) 0.03230337 0.3581461 1.290730 9.594101 uk_grouped <- polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 1, 5, 15))  head(uk_grouped$participants[, c("part_id", "part_age", "age.group")]) #>    part_id part_age age.group #>      <int>    <int>    <fctr> #> 1:    4536        0     [0,1) #> 2:    4538        0     [0,1) #> 3:    4540        0     [0,1) #> 4:    4541        0     [0,1) #> 5:    4542        0     [0,1) #> 6:    4546        0     [0,1) head(uk_grouped$contacts[, c("part_id", "cnt_age", "contact.age.group")]) #>    part_id cnt_age contact.age.group #>      <int>   <int>            <fctr> #> 1:    4517       4             [1,5) #> 2:    4517      40          [15,Inf) #> 3:    4517      31          [15,Inf) #> 4:    4517      52          [15,Inf) #> 5:    4517      29          [15,Inf) #> 6:    4517      59          [15,Inf)

text: get idea uncertainty contact matrices, participants can resampled replacement. short helper replicates participant (matching contact) rows occurrence resampled ID, duplicates preserved:
code: bootstrap <- function(survey) {   sampled_ids <- sample(     unique(survey$participants$part_id),     replace = TRUE   )   survey$participants <- survey$participants[     list(sampled_ids), on = "part_id"   ]   survey$contacts <- survey$contacts[     list(sampled_ids),     on = "part_id",     nomatch = NULL,     allow.cartesian = TRUE   ]   survey }  uk <- polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 1, 5, 15))  m <- suppressWarnings(   replicate(n = 5, uk |> bootstrap() |> compute_matrix()) ) mr <- Reduce("+", lapply(m["matrix", ], function(x) x / ncol(m))) mr #>           contact.age.group #> age.group       [0,1)     [1,5)    [5,15) [15,Inf) #>   [0,1)    0.87257409 1.3611455  1.948159 12.18693 #>   [1,5)    0.14264617 4.1499348  3.262223 10.67810 #>   [5,15)   0.08125083 1.2880114 16.003548 14.07385 #>   [15,Inf) 0.05650288 0.6471815  2.580666 19.27007

text: Obtaining symmetric contact matrices, splitting components (see ) population-based participant weights require information underlying demographic composition survey population. represented data.frame columns lower.age.limit (lower end age group) population (number people age group). can construct comparable data frame pass relevant post-processing step: recent UN World Population Prospects data, wpp2024 package available GitHub (remotes::install_github("PPgp/wpp2024")): rest section use small constructed uk_pop United Kingdom, sufficient illustrate post-processing functions:

title: Introduction to socialmixr
text: socialmixr provides small set composable functions perform one step turning survey contact matrix: survey[expr] – filter participants contacts expression. assign_age_groups() – impute missing ages assign participants contacts age groups. weigh() – optionally attach participant weights (day week, age, user-defined). compute_matrix() – compute mean contact matrix. symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita() – optional post-processing. minimal example extracting matrix UK part POLYMOD: produces contact matrix age groups 0-1, 1-5, 5-15 15+ years. contains mean number contacts member age group (row) reported members another age group (column).

title: Introduction to socialmixr
text: assign_age_groups() prepares survey matrix computation. imputes participant contact ages available ranges, drops keeps rows missing ages (configurable via missing_participant_age missing_contact_age), adds age.group contact.age.group columns using age_limits supply: resulting survey object can inspected, subset, passed number weigh() calls compute_matrix(). age_limits supplied, age groups inferred participant contact ages.

text: get idea uncertainty contact matrices, participants can resampled replacement. short helper replicates participant (matching contact) rows occurrence resampled ID, duplicates preserved: matrices, derived quantities can obtained, example mean across samples shown .
code: bootstrap <- function(survey) {   sampled_ids <- sample(     unique(survey$participants$part_id),     replace = TRUE   )   survey$participants <- survey$participants[     list(sampled_ids), on = "part_id"   ]   survey$contacts <- survey$contacts[     list(sampled_ids),     on = "part_id",     nomatch = NULL,     allow.cartesian = TRUE   ]   survey }  uk <- polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 1, 5, 15))  m <- suppressWarnings(   replicate(n = 5, uk |> bootstrap() |> compute_matrix()) ) mr <- Reduce("+", lapply(m["matrix", ], function(x) x / ncol(m))) mr #>           contact.age.group #> age.group       [0,1)     [1,5)    [5,15) [15,Inf) #>   [0,1)    0.86707602 1.2341520  1.873860 10.49181 #>   [1,5)    0.25116125 3.6745839  2.798376 10.05348 #>   [5,15)   0.05037865 0.9228788 16.525251 12.29984 #>   [15,Inf) 0.06248427 0.6102873  2.696695 18.29206

title: Introduction to socialmixr
text: Participant weights commonly used align sample population characteristics terms temporal aspects age distribution. example, day week reported driving factor social contact behaviour, hence obtain weekly average, survey data represent weekly 2/5 distribution weekend/week days. align survey data distribution, one can obtain participant weights form : wday..week=5/7Nweekday/N 2/7Nweekend/Nw_{\textrm{day..week}} = \tfrac{5/7}{N_{\textrm{weekday}}/N} \text{    } \tfrac{2/7}{N_{\textrm{weekend}}/N} sample size NN, NweekdayN_{weekday} NweekendN_{weekend} number participants surveyed weekdays weekend days, respectively. Another driver social contact patterns age. improve representativeness survey data, age-specific weights can calculated : wage=Pa/PNa/Nw_{age} = \tfrac{P_{}\ /\ P}{N_{}\ /\ N} PP population size, PaP_a population fraction age aa, NN survey sample size NaN_a survey fraction age aa. combination age-specific temporal weights participant ii age aa can constructed : wi=wage*wday..weekw_{} = w_{\textrm{age}} * w_{\textrm{day..week}} social contact analysis based stratification splitting population non-overlapping groups, requires weights standardised weighted totals within mutually exclusive cells equal known population totals (Kolenikov 2016). post-stratification cells need mutually exclusive cover whole population. compute_matrix() applies post-stratification normalisation within age groups. weigh() composable: call multiplies new weights participants’ weight column. Common recipes wrapped weigh_by_*() convenience functions: weigh_by_dayofweek() assigns weekday participants total weight 5 weekend participants total weight 2 (weekly 5/2 split). weigh_by_age() post-stratifies target population: interpolates uk_pop single-year ages pop_age() multiplies ratio target observed age share. arbitrary discrete joins, weigh() takes two-column data frame whose key column matches — e.g. pooling participants across countries target share:

NULL, age limits inferred participant contact ages. estimated_participant_age set "mean" (default), people whose ages given range (columns named "..._est_min" "..._est_max") exactly (column named "..._exact") age set mid-point range; set "sample", age sampled range; set "missing", age ranges treated missing estimated_contact_age set "mean" (default), contacts whose ages given range (columns named "..._est_min" "..._est_max") exactly (column named "..._exact") age set mid-point range; set "sample", age sampled range; set "missing", age ranges treated missing missing_participant_age set "remove" (default), participants without age information removed; set "keep", participants missing age kept treated separate age group missing_contact_age set "remove" (default), participants contacts without age information removed; set "keep", contacts missing age kept treated separate age group; set "ignore", contacts missing age ignored contact analysis. "sample" option defunct (errors). contacts age range (rather truly missing age), estimated_contact_age controls range resolved single age; substitute missing_contact_age age entirely missing.

text: Computes contact matrix contact_survey processed assign_age_groups() optionally weigh(). final step pipeline workflow. post-processing, pipe result symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita(). post-processing functions currently support single-grouping (age-) matrices.

code: compute_matrix(survey, by = "age", counts = FALSE, weight_threshold = NULL)

title: Compute contact matrix from prepared survey data — compute_matrix
text: survey survey() object columns named present participants contacts. Age groupings come assign_age_groups(); groupings already present part_<name> / cnt_<name> columns survey. character vector list grouping specifications. entry either string "age" (uses age.group / contact.age.group), stem string "<name>" (uses part_<name> / cnt_<name>), explicit c(part = "X", cnt = "Y"). Default "age" reproduces single-grouping behaviour previous releases. counts whether return counts instead means weight_threshold numeric; provided, weights threshold capped threshold value re-normalised (default NULL)

title: Compute contact matrix from prepared survey data — compute_matrix
text: contact_matrix object elements matrix (rank-2K array) participants (long table one row per grouping combination)

title: Compute contact matrix from prepared survey data — compute_matrix
text: Passing one entry produces matrix rank 2K, K = length(). first K dimensions index participants last K dimensions index contacts, order given . example, = c("age", "gender") returns array dimensions (age, gender, age, gender) — age gender participant first, contact. Dim names carry levels grouping.

title: Generate a contact matrix from diary survey data — contact_matrix
text: survey survey() object. countries limit one countries; NULL (default), use countries survey; can given country names 2-letter (ISO Alpha-2) country codes. survey_pop survey population – data frame columns lower.age.limit population. Passing NULL (default) character vector country names triggers  implicit lookup via wpp_age() symmetric, split, per_capita, weigh_age, return_demography TRUE; supply explicit data frame (e.g. constructed wpp2024 package another source) instead. age_limits lower limits age groups construct matrix. NULL (default), age limits inferred participant contact ages. filter filters apply data, given list form (column=filter_value) - contacts 'filter_value' 'column' considered. multiple filters given, applied independently sequence given. Default value NULL; filtering performed. counts whether return counts (instead means). symmetric whether make matrix symmetric, \(c_{ij}N_i = c_{ji}N_j\). split whether split contact matrix mean number contacts, age group (split product mean number contacts across whole population (mean.contacts), normalisation constant (normalisation) age-specific variation contacts (contacts)), multiplied assortativity matrix (assortativity) population multiplier (demography). detail , see "Getting Started" vignette. sample_participants whether sample participants randomly (replacement); done multiple times can used assess uncertainty generated contact matrices. title: Weigh survey participants — weigh
text: target = NULL (default) — multiply numeric column directly weight. Useful participants already carry precomputed weight column. two-column data frame whose key column named — pure discrete join: multiply value column weight key matches. Unmatched values get NA (warning). unnamed numeric vector together groups — element target total weight assigned across participants matching corresponding entry groups. per-participant factor target[g] / n_in_group. named numeric vector — names(target) matched values column. data frame target column named lower.age.limit population triggers deprecation warning falls back old hidden age post-stratification path; use weigh_by_age() instead.

title: Weigh survey participants — weigh
text: Rescales weights weekday participants together carry total weight 5 weekend participants total weight 2 — weekly 5/2 split corrects typical -representation weekdays diary surveys. Concretely, weekday participant gets 5 / n_weekday weekend participant 2 / n_weekend; participants NA day--week get neutral average 7 / N. dayofweek column taken use 0 = Sunday 6 = Saturday (POLYMOD convention). Equivalent : weigh(survey, "dayofweek", target = c(5, 2), groups = list(1:5, c(0, 6)))

title: Weigh survey participants — weigh
text: Convenience wrapper age post-stratification. main thing adds raw weigh() call interpolation: reference pop expanded single-year ages pop_age(), can supplied age resolution (e.g. 5-year bands). single-year age \(\) weight becomes $$w_a = \frac{P_a / P}{N_a / N},$$ \(P_a\) target population age \(\), \(P\) total, \(N_a\), \(N\) corresponding sample counts. survey must already processed assign_age_groups() part_age column available join.

title: socialmixr (development version)
text: compute_matrix() gains argument accepts combination participant/contact groupings, just age. entry either string "age" (matching columns produced assign_age_groups()), stem "<name>" (resolving part_<name> cnt_<name>), explicit c(part = "X", cnt = "Y") override. result rank-2K array first K axes index participants last K index contacts. default = "age" reproduces single-grouping behaviour previous releases. Post-processing functions (symmetrise(), per_capita(), split_matrix()) currently still require single-grouping matrices (#143). weigh() gains new canonical target shape: two-column data frame whose key column matches joined multiplied weight. makes recipes like weigh(survey, "country", target = ...) natural. previous silent dispatch population data frame (target data frame lower.age.limit/population column matching ) soft-deprecated; use new weigh_by_age() effect explicit name. New weigh_by_dayofweek() thin wrapper around existing 5/2 split. weigh()’s named vector groups paths unchanged (#314). Advance deprecation cycle (#312). wpp_age(), wpp_countries(), survey_country_population() deprecated (warn) — three thin layers wpp2017, way . Construct data.frame columns lower.age.limit population current source (e.g. wpp2024 package GitHub) pass contact_matrix() via survey_pop instead. implicit population lookup contact_matrix() (survey_pop given symmetric, split, per_capita, weigh_age, return_demography set) keeps warning introduced 0.6.0 sharper “error future release” message. wpp2017 package moves Imports Suggests. following now defunct (deprecate_stop): survey(), check(), get_survey(), download_survey(), list_surveys(), survey_countries(), get_citation(), dotted argument names (age.limits, survey.pop, country.column, etc.) contact_matrix(), pop_age(), clean(), as_contact_survey(), missing_contact_age = "sample" option assign_age_groups() contact_matrix(). Bogus "1.0.0" versions survey() check() deprecation messages corrected.

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-04-29 release adds pipeline composable functions building contact matrices ([, assign_age_groups(), weigh(), compute_matrix(), symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita()) contact_matrix S3 class. vignette README rewritten around pipeline (#288).

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: Minimum R version bumped 4.1.0 (3.5.0). Examples pipeline functions use native |> pipe, introduced 4.1.0. Terminal age group labels now use [N,Inf) notation instead N+ bracket notation used (e.g. [0,5), [5,15), [15,Inf)). matches contactmatrix package gives parseable interval notation across age groups. affects matrix dimnames age.group column $participants; code matches strings like "15+" need updating "[15,Inf)". Dash notation (e.g. "15+") unchanged.

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: New [.contact_survey method allows filtering survey objects expressions, e.g. polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] (#161). New weigh() function composable participant weighting: supports day--week groups, named target vectors, direct numeric columns, population post-stratification (#161). New compute_matrix() function computes contact matrix prepared survey. final step pipeline assign_age_groups() (optionally) weigh() (#161). New post-processing functions symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita() operate compute_matrix() output. symmetrise() enforces reciprocity, split_matrix() decomposes mean contacts, normalisation, assortativity matrix, per_capita() converts per-capita rates. Example workflow (#161): Pipeline functions (compute_matrix(), symmetrise(), split_matrix(), per_capita()) return contact_matrix S3 class print(), plot(), .matrix() methods. class inherits list, existing code using $matrix $participants continues work. New contact_age_distribution() function extracts empirical age distribution contacts survey. Pass assign_age_groups(estimated_contact_age = ...) impute ages ranges sampling reference distribution instead uniformly. matters surveys many contacts broad age bands, since uniform sampling flatten age-assortativity. New agegroups_to_limits() function converts age group labels back lower age limits, inverse limits_to_agegroups(). compute_matrix() gains weight_threshold parameter cap extreme weights normalisation, matching contact_matrix() option (#131).
code: uk_pop <- data.frame(   lower.age.limit = c(0, 5, 15),   population = c(3500000, 6000000, 50000000) ) polymod[country == "United Kingdom"] |>   assign_age_groups(age_limits = c(0, 5, 15)) |>   compute_matrix() |>   symmetrise(survey_pop = uk_pop)

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: Fixed bug participants NA dayofweek incorrectly weighted weekend days. now receive average weight across days (#131). Fixed unmatched-merge warning count merging files duplicate keys; previously, count wrong (negative) due counting join pairs rather distinct matched rows (#289). matrix_plot() now restores graphical parameters (par()) exit, including function errors mid-plot. Previously legend parameters (new, pty) error handler (err) left modified user’s session (#307).

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: wpp_age() wpp_countries() now soft-deprecated. Pass population data directly via survey_pop argument instead. underlying wpp2017 data also outdated; wpp2024 package GitHub provides recent data (#258). contact_matrix() now warns look population data automatically via wpp_age(). automatic lookup happens symmetric, split, per_capita, weigh_age, return_demography set countries given (participant data country column) without explicit survey_pop. implicit lookup removed future release; pass survey_pop directly (e.g. survey_country_population() wpp2024 package) silence warning make code forwards-compatible. get_survey(), download_survey(), list_surveys(), get_citation(), survey_countries() now warn unconditionally called. functions soft-deprecated 0.5.0 users switch contactsurveys package (#269).

title: socialmixr 0.6.0
text: contact_matrix() now uses assign_age_groups() internally, removing duplicated code (#227). contact_matrix() now uses weigh() internally weighting (day--week, age, user-defined). helpers warn_multiple_observations() normalise_weights() extracted compute_matrix() can share (#131). vignette README rewritten around pipeline (#288). Enabled cyclocomp_linter, line_length_linter, object_usage_linter. Disabled indentation_linter (air handles indentation). Reduced cyclomatic complexity check.contact_survey(), [.contact_survey(), find_unique_key(), try_merge_additional_files() extracting helper functions (#289).

title: socialmixr 0.5.1
text: CRAN release: 2026-02-04 patch release bug fix documentation updates. load_survey() longer fails merging contact files lack cont_id column (#278). vignette now points contactsurveys package downloading surveys Zenodo, longer uses deprecated functions (#269). Added Nicholas Tierney (@njtierney) package author (#277).

title: socialmixr 0.5.0
text: CRAN release: 2026-01-19 release focuses improved modularity flexibility contact matrix workflows. Key highlights include new standalone functions age group assignment population data retrieval, intuitive handling age limits, beginning transition contactsurveys package survey downloads.

title: socialmixr 0.5.0
text: contact_matrix() now preserves user-specified age_limits, even participants exist age groups. Previously, age groups beyond maximum participant age silently dropped. Empty age groups now show 0 participants NA values matrix. may change matrix dimensions existing code (@Bisaloo, #144, #231). contact_matrix(counts = TRUE)$matrix now returns array rather xtabs object. matches existing output format contact_matrix(counts = FALSE)$matrix (@Bisaloo, #118). age_limits specified, now inferred participant contact ages, just participant ages. may result age groups contacts include ages beyond participant age range (#230).

title: socialmixr 0.5.0
text: as_contact_survey() longer requires country year columns. columns now auto-detected present, surveys without can loaded successfully (#193, #199). New assign_age_groups() survey_country_population() functions allow modular pre-processing survey data (#131, #226). Reduced verbosity removing messages removing participants/contacts missing ages (#228).

title: socialmixr 0.5.0
text: clean() now correctly processes age values units (e.g., “6 months”, “52 weeks”) (@LloydChapman, #250, #256). contact_matrix() now warns survey contains multiple observations per participant, results aggregate across observations (#260). load_survey() now correctly loads longitudinal surveys repeated observations per participant (e.g., sday files wave/studyDay columns). Previously, columns silently dropped (@njtierney, #192, #194). Fixed bug leading excess contacts NA age lowest age group start 0 (@lwillem, #170).

title: socialmixr 0.5.0
text: Argument names dots (e.g., age.limits) deprecated favour underscores (e.g., age_limits) contact_matrix(), as_contact_survey(), pop_age(), clean(). old argument names still work produce deprecation warnings (#160). get_survey(), download_survey(), get_citation(), list_surveys(), survey_countries() soft-deprecated moved contactsurveys. part decoupling features socialmixr reduce dependencies (@njtierney, #179, #207). continue work version 1.0.0. missing_contact_age = "sample" option contact_matrix() assign_age_groups() soft-deprecated. Use "remove" exclude contacts missing ages, "keep" retain separate age group, "ignore" drop contacts (#273).
